title: Dracula janetiae
taxonomy: Dracula janetiae is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. It is native to Colombia and Ecuador in South America. The genus Dracula is a group of orchids that are often referred to as the "vampire orchids" due to their unique characteristics. Dracula janetiae is part of the tribe Masdewieae within the subfamily Epidendroideae. This classification places Dracula janetiae in the larger family of orchids.
benefits: Dracula janetiae is known to have potential health benefits due to its unique properties. The plant is rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Further research is needed to fully understand the health benefits of Dracula janetiae.

Its roots contain a compound called iridoid glycoside, which has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. constituents: Dracula janetiae contains several active constituents, including iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids.
